source_context:
CV Assistant implements an end-to-end research automation pipeline from paper 
discovery to structured knowledge.

Stage 1 – Discovery: 
The monitor module polls ArXiv, Papers With Code, and Semantic Scholar continuously.
New papers matching configured topics are queued automatically.

Stage 2 – Paper Fetch:
For each queued paper, the agent fetches the abstract, PDF, and metadata.
Full PDF text is extracted for deep analysis.

Stage 3 – Multi-faceted Extraction:
The agent applies several extraction passes:
- Mathematical equations (LaTeX → structured JSON)
- Architecture descriptions (model components, layers, connections)
- Dataset references (name, splits, metrics)
- Benchmark numbers (tables → structured data)
- Key contributions and novelty claims

Stage 4 – Spec Generation:
A spec.md file is generated for each paper:
problem statement, method, architecture diagram description,
equations, datasets, metrics, reproducibility notes.

Stage 5 – Knowledge Graph Indexing:
Paper nodes, concept nodes, and equation nodes are added to an 
Obsidian vault. Edges encode "uses", "improves-on", "cites", "applies-to" relationships.
The graph grows incrementally and supports semantic queries.

Stage 6 – Weekly Digest:
Every Monday the digest job queries the knowledge graph for the week's papers,
ranks by citation count and novelty score, and generates a Markdown blog post
summarising breakthroughs, architecture trends, and benchmark moves.
communicative_intent: Research-to-knowledge pipeline: automated paper discovery → multi-faceted extraction → spec generation → knowledge graph → weekly digest.
